entries.observation: The bid-ask-spread / order-flow-slippage lesson was taught in 6 of 8 recent episodes with nearly identical framing. Listeners tuned out after the third repeat.
entries.adjustment: Never re-teach bid_ask_spread or order_flow_slippage within their 45-day cooldown. If the only interesting mechanic today is a stale one, pick a different mechanic — freshness beats any single lesson.

entries.observation: The last 10 picks skewed 35% toward precious metals (Fortuna, SSR, West Red Lake) with zero flag raised on air. A real portfolio would have rebalanced.
entries.adjustment: Cap any single sector at 30% of the trailing 10-trade window. When the sector-concentration warning fires, the Practice Investment MUST pick a different sector that episode.

entries.observation: No episode has ever named the portfolio's alpha versus the NASDAQ Composite. Listeners have no way to judge whether the show is beating the market.
entries.adjustment: Every episode must state NASDAQ Composite level, portfolio YTD return, and YTD alpha in the Market Pulse segment. Fridays add the weekly alpha. Monthly episodes add month-to-date alpha.

entries.observation: Quick Hits consistently slipped into pure news reading (maple syrup shortage, Ackman's Universal Music bid, craft-brewer cost absorption) with no investing angle.
entries.adjustment: Every Quick Hit ends with an **Action:** line containing exactly one concrete portfolio decision stated as a verb phrase. Items without a concrete action are rejected at the digest stage.
